declared_deviations.reason: After 4 independently-refuted attempts this session to DERIVE Delta_j/alpha/beta from the root grammar (see domains/standard_model/item1_exploration/ITEM1_EXPLORATION_LOG.md), the founder directed a deliberate, openly-declared methodology change: FIT Delta_j/alpha/beta the way the real Standard Model itself is built -- ~19+ free parameters measured/fit to data, not derived from a deeper theory. This is standard, legitimate physics methodology, not a shortcut; the founder's own words: do not demand more of this program than the Standard Model itself delivers.
declared_deviations.risk: The exact circularity intertwiner_order_vacuum_v1_13.py's own INT-N6 negative control warns about: fitting lambda_j to reproduce known fermion-mass phenomenology and then citing the resulting Pi0>alpha as if it were evidence the order criterion is FORCED by the root, when it was tuned to produce that answer. INT-N6 is NOT weakened or repealed by this deviation -- it still correctly forbids exactly that (an UNDECLARED or mislabeled fit masquerading as a derivation).
compensating_controls: every fitted value MUST be tagged tier fit_calibrated (a new tier, distinct from Th_coqc/finite_diagnostic/Dr/Open -- 'matches an SM_INFORMATION_PHILOSOPHY_MASTER.md, section-21-style closed/exact tier, this is explicitly NOT that')

every use of a fit_calibrated value or any Pi0 computed from one MUST carry the caveat 'FITTED, not derived from the root; consistent-with, not forced-by' in the SAME sentence/line it is first reported, and in every downstream citation, not just the origin file

this is declared OPTIONAL, not a new default: Th_coqc/Dr-tier derivation attempts remain the primary, preferred track for item 1; fit_calibrated is a parallel, explicitly-secondary track, not a replacement
a fit_calibrated result MUST NOT be used to claim end-to-end root-derived Standard Model closure (hard_fail_conditions[6] still applies in full)
INT-N6 itself is NOT edited or weakened in intertwiner_order_vacuum_v1_13.py -- it continues to correctly forbid the undeclared/circular version of this same move
declared_deviations.approved_by: founder (yaoharee.lt), 2026-07-24, explicit instruction: "แก้กฎเปิดเผยก่อน แล้วไป (ก) และให้เป็นข้อยกเว้นว่าฟิตได้แต่ต้องประกาศ และให้ถือว่าเป็นออฟชั่น"

hard_fail_conditions: importing SU(3), SU(2), U(1), or SU(3)xSU(2)xU(1) as a PREMISE (they may appear only as a discovered/labelled endpoint, in comparison text, or as a semantic label attached AFTER a derivation node)
importing quark, lepton, Higgs, gauge-boson, or any named particle as a premise
importing a named gauge equation (Yang-Mills action, Dirac equation, Higgs potential) as a premise
starting from a gauge group instead of from readout-preserving automorphisms Aut(F,O)

claiming a gauge group, representation set, generation count, mixing angle, or coupling constant is derived from the UNRESTRICTED root (finite/minimal-architecture closures ARE allowed and must say 'exact within the declared architecture', never 'derived from the root' unqualified)
reading AP20's self-interaction ratio or AP10's beta slopes as a root-derivation of the Standard Model
any claim that end-to-end root-derived Standard Model is > 0%

claiming this folder has a single end-to-end Standard Model verifier (it does not, by design); it DOES have node-level verifiers + Coq witnesses (run_tests.py), and that is a real, checkable, PASS/Closed status per node -- do not deny their existence and do not conflate node-level PASS with end-to-end closure
mixing the derivation chain and the empirical-discovery chain
calling a local Hessian / curvature at a minimum a PHYSICAL POLE MASS without a spectral-transfer certificate
calling a calibrated decoder (e.g. electroweak_decoder_v0_3) a PREDICTION rather than a calibration-consistency check

citing the SUPERSEDED exponential order-closure ansatz (Z_j=1+zeta e^{kappa r}) as a canonical or active result; the canonical order-vacuum result is intertwiner_order_vacuum_v1_13 (Fock factor (1+lambda r)^d, Z_j(0)=1)

declaring the order criterion Pi0>alpha PROVEN/FORCED (i.e. root-derived) before the primitive costs (Delta_j, alpha, beta) are derived from the root grammar -- it is currently a conditional criterion only. See declared_deviations[0] for the ONE explicitly-permitted exception: a FITTED (not derived) Delta_j/alpha/beta may be used and Pi0 computed from it, IF AND ONLY IF every occurrence is tagged fit_calibrated and explicitly disclaims 'forced'/'proven'/'derived from the root'. An undisclosed fit, or a disclosed fit later cited elsewhere without its tag and caveat, both remain hard-fail violations of this condition.
note: The Standard Model is a domain-translation to be DISCOVERED, never a root. The gauge group is a semantic label added only after group identification from data (the discovery chain).
prohibited_active_premise_tokens_scope: The token list below is a PREMISE-POSITION rule only: forbidden as an input assumption feeding a derivation. The SAME tokens are ALLOWED as: (a) a discovered/labelled endpoint after a derivation (e.g. 'the recovered algebra IS su(3)+su(2)+u(1)'), (b) a comparison to known physics for honesty/calibration, (c) a semantic label attached AFTER a blind search (e.g. v1.6's matter skeleton labels Q,u^c,d^c,L,e^c only after the search).
